rulings.Case Title: GREG SCHOEPP, DBA BAY AREA COMPASSION HEALTH CARE VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, BY AND THROUGH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion To Strike Or Tax Costs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 8, 2012, Line 5. PETITIONER GREG SCHOEPP, DBA BAY AREA COMPASSION HEALTH CARE CENTER, INC.'S NOTICE Of Motion To Strike Or Tax Costs. Granted in part. The filing fee is considered a cost incurred under the law that shall be included in the Judgment and the Court does not have discretion to tax such cost. The cost for preparation of the administrative record is also recoverable, but should be reduced by $250 to account for the deposit paid by petitioner. Accordingly, the City is awarded costs in the amount of $410 in filing fees and $288 for preparation of the administrative record. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION INC., VS. PARMINDER S. CHAHAL INDIVIDUALLY DBA MONTEREY MINI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order; Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment; Application For Right To Attach Order; Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment After Hearing And Pos (Akbar Matani Ind Dba Monterey Mini Mart)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 8, 2012, Line 1, PLAINTIFF CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION INC.'s Hearing For Right To Attach Order (Akbar Matani Individually and DBA Monterey Mini Mart). Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL BRADFORD et al VS. MARTIN, FRED et al*****CASE TRANSFERRED TO SANTA CLARA COUNTY*****
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 8, 2012, Line 11. DEFENDANTS NANCY MARTIN AND FRED MARTIN'S MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE TO EL DORADO COUNTY. Granted as to change of venue and denied as to fees and costs. The case is transferred to El Dorado County. Defendants have shown that El Dorado County was their county of residence when plaintiffs commenced this action. Plaintiffs fail to show that the leases were executed in San Francisco and defendants' objection to the leases as not properly authenticated is sustained. Plaintiff had a good faith basis to file the action in San Francisco given the address of defendants' condominium. Per Cubic, defendants are entitled to have the case transferred to El Dorado County. Plaintiffs can seek to change venue to Santa Clara County by filing a convenience of witnesses motion in El Dorado County after defendants file an answer. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: STATE FARM MUTUAL AUTOMOBILE INSURANCE COMPANY VS. KAT M LO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 8, 2012, Line 17. DEFENDANT YU GUAN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend. A subrogated insurer "has no greater rights than the insured." (Travelers Cas. & Sur. Co. v. American Equity Ins. Co., (2001) 93 Cal.App.4th 1142, 1151.) Plaintiff's insured has no right against the Defendants because her previous lawsuit regarding this same matter was dismissed on July 7, 2005. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LEONARD M. GEYER VS. ROBERT DREYER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 8, 2012, Line 16. DEFENDANTS ROBERT DREYER, ROBERT DREYER INC, ACCOUNTANT, A CORPORATION, CRISTINA DREYER INC, ACCOUNTANT, A CORPORATION, DAVID LUCHETTI, DAVID LUCHETTI INC, ACCOUNTANT, A CORPORATION, DREYER, GEYER, LUCHETTI INC., ACCOUNTANT, AND CHRISTINA DREYER'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Overruled as to the tenth cause of action, sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the fifth and seventh causes of action and sustained without leave to amend as to the ninth and eleventh cause of action. The civil embezzlement claim is adequately pled. The 17200 claim does not adequately alleged any unlawful, unfair or fraudulent business practice, and plaintiffs are given leave to cure this deficiency if they can do so in good faith. The fraud claim does not plead all of the elements of actionable fraud with the required particularity, and plaintiffs are given leave to cure this deficiency if they can do so in good faith. The conversion claim is deficient because it does not allege the right to immediately possess a specific sum of money, and it does not appear that plaintiffs can cure this deficiency. The intentional infliction of emotional distress claim is deficient because it does not allege extreme and outrageous conduct, and it does not appear that plaintiffs can cure this deficiency. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)
